title: Cavallo & Bertolotto — Complete Argentina Inflation Series 1943-2016+ (Filling the Gap in Argentina's Inflation Data): paper AND the underlying reconstructed price-index dataset
source_org: Alberto Cavallo (MIT Sloan / NBER / PriceStats) and Manuel Bertolotto (PriceStats / UdeSA)
source_url: https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=2787276 (SSRN abstract — blocked by Cloudflare bot-challenge, HTTP 403 on direct fetch); paper PDF originally hosted at https://www.thebillionpricesproject.com/wp-content/papers/FillingTheGap.pdf (now HTTP 500 on live site); underlying data originally hosted at https://www.inflacionverdadera.com/Argentina_inflation.csv (domain now repurposed as an unrelated WordPress site, 404 on live URL)
download_url: http://web.archive.org/web/20250118091926/https://www.thebillionpricesproject.com/wp-content/papers/FillingTheGap.pdf (paper, via Wayback Machine) ; http://web.archive.org/web/20220901054436/http://www.inflacionverdadera.com/Argentina_inflation.csv (DATA, via Wayback Machine) ; https://www.hbs.edu/ris/download.aspx?name=Cavallo_Alberto_Online+and+Official+Price+Indexes+Measuring+Argentinas+Inflation.pdf (related precursor paper, live HBS server, direct download)

files.name: Cavallo_OnlineOfficialPriceIndexes_JME2013.pdf
files.original_name: Cavallo_Alberto_Online and Official Price Indexes Measuring Argentinas Inflation.pdf

time_coverage: Argentina_inflation csv: monthly index level, month-over-month % and year-over-year % inflation, 1943m1 to 2018m11 (912 rows) — extends beyond the paper's nominal '1943-2016' title because the series was kept updated monthly per the paper's stated methodology until the site went dark; papers: FillingTheGap draft dated May 2016; JME 2013 paper covers the 2007-2011 online-vs-official comparison period specifically.
units: CPI index level (chained, arbitrary base far in the past given tiny early values reflecting cumulative multi-decade inflation) and % monthly/annual inflation rate
license_terms: Academic working paper + author-hosted data; no explicit license stated on the original site; used here for research/reference per the project's documentation purpose
notes: HIGH-VALUE FIND — this is the actual underlying dataset behind the Cavallo-Bertolotto reconstruction, not just the paper. Chain: (1) SSRN abstract page (2787276) is Cloudflare-protected and returns HTTP 403 to automated fetches — could not retrieve via SSRN directly (confirmed genuinely bot-blocked, not merely paywalled — SSRN itself indicates the paper has free full-text download for registered/no-login users normally, but the anti-bot challenge could not be passed here). (2) The paper's own text was located instead via the author's institutional site www.thebillionpricesproject.com/wp-content/papers/FillingTheGap.pdf, whose live URL now 500-errors, so retrieved via Wayback Machine snapshot (2025-01-18) instead — full text confirmed matching (title 'Filling the Gap in Argentina's Inflation Data', Cavallo & Bertolotto, 'This Draft: May 2016', abstract matches SSRN listing). (3) Section 4 of the paper ('How to download the data') states verbatim: 'The full CPI series is available for download at www.inflacionverdadera.com/Argentina_inflation.csv'. That exact URL now 404s (domain inflacionverdadera.com has been repurposed as an unrelated WordPress blog) — retrieved instead via Wayback Machine's last live-content snapshot (2022-09-01; CDX history shows the file was stable/unchanged from at least 2020-05-23 through 2022-09-01, then the domain went dead by 2022-10). (4) Also retrieved the closely related precursor paper 'Online and Official Price Indexes: Measuring Argentina's Inflation' (Cavallo, Journal of Monetary Economics 2013) directly from HBS's own RIS server (live, HTTP 200) as supporting/background material — this is the peer-reviewed journal article that established the online-price methodology later extended to the full 1943-2016 series. No Harvard Dataverse entry specifically for this Argentina series was found; Cavallo's Harvard Dataverse entries (e.g. doi:10.7910/DVN/6RQCRS, the Billion Prices Project JEP-2016 dataset) cover different multi-country online price index data (2007/2008-2015, 8 countries) and were checked but do not contain the 1943-2016 Argentina series specifically.

failures.note: SSRN returned Cloudflare bot-challenge (cf-mitigated: challenge) on both WebFetch and direct curl with browser User-Agent — could not retrieve abstract page or PDF via SSRN directly.
Live URL cited by search engines now returns Internal Server Error; recovered via Wayback Machine snapshot instead.
Domain now repurposed as unrelated WordPress site; the exact data URL cited in the paper's Section 4 no longer resolves live; recovered via Wayback Machine snapshot (2022-09-01) instead.
